# Watchdog Setup — One-Time Steps

## What this does
The watchdog runs 90 minutes after each scheduled Job Search Bot window.
If the bot didn't run (GitHub dropped it), the watchdog re-triggers it automatically.

Schedule:
  - 06:30 UTC → checks if 05:00 UTC (08:00 Israel) ran
  - 10:30 UTC → checks if 09:00 UTC (12:00 Israel) ran
  - 15:30 UTC → checks if 14:00 UTC (17:00 Israel) ran
  - 19:30 UTC → checks if 18:00 UTC (21:00 Israel) ran

---

## Step 1 — Create a GitHub Personal Access Token

1. Go to: https://github.com/settings/tokens/new
2. Note: "Job Bot Watchdog"
3. Expiration: 1 year (or No expiration)
4. Scopes — check ONLY:
   ✅ repo → workflow   (to trigger workflows)
   ✅ repo → actions:read  (to list runs)
   
   (Easiest: just check the top-level "repo" box)
5. Click "Generate token"
6. COPY the token now — you won't see it again

---

## Step 2 — Add it as a GitHub Secret

1. Go to your repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions
2. Click "New repository secret"
3. Name:  WATCHDOG_TOKEN
4. Value: paste the token from Step 1
5. Click "Add secret"

---

## Step 3 — Add the workflow file

Copy watchdog.yml to:
  .github/workflows/watchdog.yml

Then push:
  git add .github/workflows/watchdog.yml
  git commit -m "feat: add watchdog to re-trigger missed bot runs"
  git push

---

## Step 4 — Verify it works

After pushing, go to:
  Actions → Job Bot Watchdog → Run workflow (manually)

Check the log — it should say either:
  ✅ Job Search Bot ran recently — no action needed
  ⚠️ No recent run found — triggering Job Search Bot now
